suggestions.title: Inline style object in completionToast creates new object every render
suggestions.description: The completionToast notification in App.jsx uses a massive inline style={{...}} object directly in JSX. This creates a new object reference every render cycle, defeating React.memo and causing unnecessary re-renders of the entire App tree. Extract to a CSS class or useMemo the style object.

suggestions.title: vibeStreak prop not passed to Office component
suggestions.description: App.jsx computes vibeStreak state but the <Office> component receives it as a prop for the Fern plant growth stages. Need to verify the prop is actually being passed — grep shows it IS passed, but the plant stage calculation uses Math.floor(vibeStreak / 5) which means the plant won't grow past seedling until streak=5. Consider lowering the threshold so users see growth sooner.

suggestions.title: Tab panel switching lacks ARIA live region announcement
suggestions.description: When switching between Office and Dashboard tabs, screen readers get no announcement that content has changed. The tabpanel containers should have aria-live='polite' or use a visually hidden status message to announce the switch. Also, the tab role buttons don't manage focus correctly — after arrow key navigation, focus should move to the newly selected tab.

suggestions.title: useTimeTick(60000) causes full App re-render every minute
suggestions.description: The useTimeTick hook in App.jsx increments a counter every 60s to keep relative timestamps fresh. But since it's called at the App level, it forces the ENTIRE component tree to re-render every minute — including Office, all WorkerCards, StatsBar, etc. Move this hook into individual components that display relative times (ActivityLogEntry, WorkerCard duration) or use a context provider with selective subscriptions.

suggestions.title: vibe history localStorage race condition
suggestions.description: In App.jsx, vibeHistory is read from localStorage in StatsBar's useEffect AND written in App's useEffect. Both fire on teamVibe.key change. The StatsBar read can get stale data if App's write hasn't flushed yet. Consolidate vibe history management into one place (App) and pass the array down as a prop.

suggestions.title: Mobile viewport: character names hidden but no alternative identification
suggestions.description: At ≤480px, .char__name { display: none } hides character names in the office view. But there's no alternative way to identify who's who — the avatars are tiny and hard to distinguish. Consider showing a single-letter badge or keeping names visible at a smaller font size instead of hiding them entirely.

suggestions.title: ROLE_EMOJIS in constants.js has 'QA Engineer' but Sofia's role is 'QA & Product Analyst'
suggestions.description: Sofia's role string in workers.json is 'QA & Product Analyst' but ROLE_EMOJIS and ROLE_COLORS only have 'QA Engineer'. This means Sofia gets the fallback emoji (🤖) and default gray color (#6b7280) in the dashboard WorkerCards. Add 'QA & Product Analyst' to both maps, or normalize the role string.

suggestions.title: AwaySign component recalculates message on every render
suggestions.description: AwaySign selects its message using Math.floor(idleMinutes / 10) which changes every 10 minutes — fine. But the component is re-rendered every time the parent desk re-renders (every poll cycle). The string splitting logic (reduce for line1/line2) runs unnecessarily. Wrap in React.memo or useMemo the computed values.

suggestions.title: Conference table positioned at left:38% with translate(-50%) — visually off-center
suggestions.description: The CSS for .conference-table uses left:38% + transform:translate(-50%,-50%). This positions the table's center at 38% from the left, which is noticeably left-of-center. Should be left:50% for true centering, or adjust to ~45% if intentionally offset to avoid the door.

suggestions.title: No favicon fallback for light theme
suggestions.description: The dynamic favicon generation uses solid colors on a transparent canvas. In light browser themes, the grey idle state (#9ca3af) can be hard to see against the tab bar. Add a subtle dark ring/border to the favicon circle for contrast.

suggestions.title: Dead CSS selectors referencing old class names
suggestions.description: Office.css has selectors for .office-worker, .office-worker--ghost, .office-worker--manager, and .worker-standby inside the prefers-reduced-motion block, but these classes don't exist anywhere in the JSX — the actual classes are .char, .char--ghost, .char--manager. These dead selectors should be cleaned up.
